title: NORMALIZE_NAMES scope needs reviewing
description:
according to the documentation
"""
normalize_names	Normalize column names. This removes any non-alphanumeric characters from them. Column names that are reserved SQL keywords are prefixed with an underscore character (_).
"""

```
example: (I would have expected **_commit** and **_rollback** as they are (typically) seen as reserved keywords 
REFERENCE: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_SQL_reserved_words

create table places as select * from read_csv("pop.csv", normalize_names=True);
select * from places;
```

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, _select VARCHAR, _join VARCHAR, commit INT, rollback VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, _select, _join, commit, rollback) VALUES (1, 'Test', 'Join', 100, NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, _select, _join, commit, rollback) VALUES (2, 'Example', 'Join', NULL, 'Rollback');
SELECT id, _select, _join, commit, rollback FROM test_table WHERE commit IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE another_table AS SELECT id, _select, _join FROM test_table WHERE _select LIKE 'T%';
key_bug_features: Normalization of reserved SQL keywords in column names
